questions.text: Type sizes are usually expressed in ________.
questions.explanation: In typography, type sizes are traditionally measured in points. There are approximately 72 points to an inch.

questions.text: When a password is entered in upper or lower case to match the original password, it is said to be ________.
questions.explanation: Case sensitivity refers to the system's ability to distinguish between uppercase and lowercase characters.

questions.text: Symbolic representations of objects and processes common to the graphical user interfaces of many computer operating systems are called ________.
questions.explanation: Icons are small graphic symbols used in GUIs to represent files, folders, applications, or functions.

questions.text: Special HTML characters, always prefixed by an ampersand (escape) and followed by a semicolon, are called ________.
questions.explanation: Character entities are used to display reserved characters or symbols in HTML that cannot easily be typed or would be interpreted as code.

questions.text: WYSIWYG stands for ________.

choices.label: A
choices.text: What You See Is What You Get

choices.label: B
choices.text: Why You See Is What You Get

choices.label: C
choices.text: Where You See Is What You Get

choices.label: D
choices.text: When You See Is What You Get

questions.explanation: WYSIWYG describes a system in which the content displayed during editing appears very similar to the final output.

questions.text: Translating or designing multimedia into a language other than the one in which it was originally written is called ________.
questions.explanation: Localization involves adapting a product, including language and cultural nuances, for a specific locale or market.

questions.text: The little decoration at the end of a letter stroke is a ________.
questions.explanation: Serifs are the small lines or strokes regularly attached to the end of a larger stroke in a letter or symbol within a particular font.

questions.text: Designers call roomy blank areas ________.
questions.explanation: White space (often called negative space) refers to the portions of a page left unmarked to improve readability and aesthetics.

questions.text: ________ blends the colors along the edges of the letters to create a soft transition between the letter and its background.
questions.explanation: Anti-aliasing is a technique used to smooth the jagged edges of digital images or text by blending the pixels at the boundaries.

questions.text: Conceptual elements, containing text, graphics, sounds, or related information in the knowledge base, are called ________.
questions.explanation: In hypertext and hypermedia, a node is a basic unit of information that can contain various media types.

questions.text: A family of graphic characters that usually includes many type sizes and styles is called a:
questions.explanation: A typeface is the design of the letterforms, while a font is the specific delivery mechanism (like a file) of that typeface at a certain size/style.

questions.text: Which of the following is a term that applies to the spacing between character pairs?
questions.explanation: Kerning is the process of adjusting the spacing between individual letter pairs to achieve a visually pleasing result.

questions.text: Intercap, the practice of placing a capital in the middle of a word, is a trend that emerged from the computer programming community:

choices.label: A
choices.text: to copy marketing practices in the electronics industry.

choices.label: B
choices.text: to better see the words used by programmers for variables and commands.

choices.label: C
choices.text: because one of the first computer programmers had a faulty Shift key.

choices.label: D
choices.text: to increase security in case sensitive passwords.

questions.explanation: Intercapping (CamelCase) was adopted by programmers to make multi-word variable names legible without spaces.

questions.text: Dynamic HTML uses ________ to define choices ranging from line height to margin width to font face.
questions.explanation: Cascading Style Sheets (CSS) allow developers to define the layout and styling of HTML elements dynamically.

questions.text: If a DHTML document includes a font face that is not installed on the user's computer, a browser will:

choices.label: D
choices.text: try to substitute the font with a similar font.

questions.explanation: Browsers use a fallback mechanism where they try to match the requested font with available system fonts or generic families.

questions.text: In the URL `http://www.timestream.com/info/people/biotay/biotay1.html`, which part is case sensitive?
questions.explanation: While domain names are case-insensitive, the path and filename on a web server (especially Unix-based ones) are often case-sensitive.

questions.text: Multimedia becomes interactive multimedia when:

choices.label: A
choices.text: the user has some control over what information is viewed and when it is viewed.

choices.label: B
choices.text: the information is displayed by a computer with a touchscreen or other input device.

choices.label: C
choices.text: the information is available on the Web, either the Internet or a Local Area Network.

choices.label: D
choices.text: quizzes and tests with evaluations and scoring are included.

questions.explanation: Interactivity is defined by the end-user's ability to manipulate or control the sequence and timing of content delivery.

questions.text: Interactive multimedia becomes hypermedia when:

choices.label: A
choices.text: the information is available on the Web, either the Internet or a Local Area Network.

choices.label: B
choices.text: quizzes and tests with evaluations and scoring are included.

choices.label: C
choices.text: it includes a structure of linked elements through which a user can navigate and interact.

choices.label: D
choices.text: the user can change such attributes as volume and type size.

questions.explanation: Hypermedia is the extension of hypertext that includes links between non-linear multimedia elements.

questions.text: Web pages are coded using:
questions.explanation: HTML is the standard markup language used to create and structure pages on the World Wide Web.

questions.text: Which of the following provides a system for dynamically displaying a font?
questions.explanation: PostScript is a page description language used in electronic and desktop publishing to describe the appearance of a printed page including fonts.

questions.text: A printed page might be presented in which of these orientations?
questions.explanation: Portrait orientation is where the height of the page is greater than its width, which is standard for most documents.

questions.text: Which of the following is a character encoding system?
questions.explanation: Unicode is a universal character encoding standard that provides a unique number for every character across different platforms and languages.

questions.text: The reference from one document to another document, image, sound, or file on the Web is a(n):
questions.explanation: In HTML, the `<a>` tag stands for anchor and is used to create hyperlinks to other resources.

questions.text: What is a general measurement applied to individual characters called?
questions.explanation: Character metrics are the set of parameters that describe the size and shape of a character, such as width and height.

questions.text: Which of the following is a typical method for word searching in a hypermedia system?
questions.explanation: Adjacency search allows users to search for phrases or words that appear near each other in a document.

questions.text: Type sizes are usually expressed in which unit?
questions.explanation: In typography, font size is traditionally measured in points. One point is approximately 1/72 of an inch.

questions.text: When a password must match the original password's capitalization exactly, it is said to be:
questions.explanation: Case sensitivity refers to the computer's ability to distinguish between uppercase and lowercase characters.

questions.text: What are the symbolic representations of objects and processes common to graphical user interfaces (GUIs) called?
questions.explanation: Icons are small graphic representations of programs, files, or functions used in GUIs to make navigation more intuitive.

questions.text: Special HTML characters that are prefixed by an ampersand and followed by a semicolon are known as:
questions.explanation: Character entities are used in HTML to display reserved characters or characters not present on a standard keyboard (e.g., \© for the copyright symbol).

questions.text: The acronym WYSIWYG stands for:

choices.label: A
choices.text: What You See Is What You Get

choices.label: B
choices.text: Web Yields Simple Integrated Web Graphics

choices.label: C
choices.text: Where You Select Is Where You Go

choices.label: D
choices.text: Work You Save Is Work You Gain

questions.explanation: WYSIWYG describes an editor where the content displayed during editing appears very similar to the final output.

questions.text: What is the term for translating or designing multimedia into a language and culture other than its original one?
questions.explanation: Localization involves adapting a product to a specific locale, which includes translation and cultural adjustments.

questions.text: In typography, the little decoration at the end of a letter stroke is called a:
questions.explanation: A serif is a small line or stroke attached to the end of a larger stroke in a letter within a particular font family.

questions.text: What term do designers use to describe roomy blank areas in a layout?
questions.explanation: White space (often called negative space) refers to the portions of a page left unmarked, helping to reduce clutter and improve readability.

questions.text: Which technique blends colors along the edges of letters to create a soft transition between the letter and its background?
questions.explanation: Anti-aliasing smooths the jagged edges of digital images or text by averaging the colors of the pixels at the boundary.

questions.text: What are conceptual elements containing text, graphics, or sounds in a knowledge base called?
questions.explanation: In hypermedia systems, a node is a basic unit of information that can contain various media and is connected to others via links.

questions.text: Which of the following is a term that applies to the spacing between character pairs?
questions.explanation: Kerning is the process of adjusting the spacing between specific pairs of characters to achieve a visually pleasing result.

questions.text: Intercap, the practice of placing a capital in the middle of a word, is a trend that emerged from the computer programming community:

choices.label: B
choices.text: to copy marketing practices in the electronics industry.

choices.label: C
choices.text: to better see the words used by programmers for variables and commands.

choices.label: D
choices.text: to increase security in case sensitive passwords.

questions.explanation: Also known as CamelCase, Intercap allows programmers to join multiple words into a single identifier while maintaining readability.

questions.text: Interactive multimedia becomes hypermedia when:

choices.label: A
choices.text: the information is available on the Web.

choices.label: B
choices.text: quizzes and tests with evaluations are included.

choices.label: C
choices.text: it includes a structure of linked elements through which a user can navigate.

choices.label: D
choices.text: the user can change attributes such as volume.

questions.explanation: Hypermedia is multimedia that utilizes a linked structure (hyperlinks) to allow non-linear navigation between information nodes.

questions.text: Which of the following is a typical method for word searching in a hypermedia system?
questions.explanation: Adjacency search allows users to search for two or more words that appear next to each other in a specific order.
